# Dropbox Connector - Full Documentation

# Dropbox Connector 1.0 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Configurations

---

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Config_Oauth2">Oauth2 Connection Provider</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Connection types for this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td><p>AUTO</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Oauth2 Connection Provider

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a>.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultQueryParam">Default Query Param</a>.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether to use persistent connections:</p><p>* <code>true</code></p><p>Mule uses persistent connections.</p><p>* <code>false</code></p><p>Mule closes the connection after the first request completes.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of connections to open to the backend. HTTP requests are sent in parallel over multiple connections. Setting this value too high can impact latency and consume additional resources without increasing throughput.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>When persistent connections are enabled, how long a connection can remain idle before Mule closes it.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Idle Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If this value is <code>true</code>, Mule streams received responses.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Size of the buffer that stores the HTTP response, in bytes.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Token Access Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>offline</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Protocol to use for communication.</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>What to do if, when an app is deployed, a connectivity test does not pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy:</p><p>* <code>true</code></p><p>Allow the deployment to fail.</p><p>* <code>false</code></p><p>Ignore the results of the connectivity test.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth consumer key, as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth consumer secret, as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorization Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL of the service providerâs authorization endpoint.</p></td><td><p><code>https://www.dropbox.com/oauth2/authorize</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL of the service providerâs access token endpoint.</p></td><td><p><code>https://api.dropboxapi.com/oauth2/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance.</p></td><td><p>account_info.write, account_info.read, files.metadata.write, files.metadata.read, files.content.write, files.content.read, sharing.write, sharing.read, file_requests.write, file_requests.read, contacts.write, files.permanent_delete</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Owner Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Resource owner ID to use with the authorization code grant type.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the flow to execute immediately before starting the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>After</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the flow to execute immediately after receiving an access token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Listener Config</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A reference to an <code>&lt;http:listener-config /&gt;</code> to use to create the listener that will catch the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Callback Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path of the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorize Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path of the local HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>External Callback Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL that the OAuth provider uses to access the callback endpoint if the endpoint is behind a proxy or accessed through an indirect URL.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the object store that stores data for each resource owner. If not configured, Mule uses the default object store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Files Delete V2](#FilesDeleteV2)
- [Files Download](#FilesDownload)
- [Files List Folder](#FilesListFolder)
- [Files List Revisions](#FilesListRevisions)
- [Files Move Batch V2](#FilesMoveBatchV2)
- [Files Move V2](#FilesMoveV2)
- [Files Restore](#FilesRestore)
- [Files Search V2](#FilesSearchV2)
- [Files Upload](#FilesUpload)
- [Sharing Add File Member](#SharingAddFileMember)
- [Sharing Add Folder Member](#SharingAddFolderMember)
- [Sharing List File Members](#SharingListFileMembers)
- [Sharing List Folder Members](#SharingListFolderMembers)
- [Sharing Remove File Member2](#SharingRemoveFileMember2)
- [Sharing Remove Folder Member](#SharingRemoveFolderMember)
- [Sharing Share Folder](#SharingShareFolder)
- [Unauthorize](#Unauthorize)
- [Users Get Current Account](#UsersGetCurrentAccount)
- [Users Get Space Usage](#UsersGetSpaceUsage)

## Sources

- [On create or update file in folder](#OnNewOrUpdatedFileInFolderListener)

## File Requests Create

`<dropbox:file-requests-create>`

Creates a file request for this user. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/file_requests/create endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- DROPBOX:BAD_REQUEST
- DROPBOX:CLIENT_ERROR
- DROPBOX:CONNECTIVITY
- DROPBOX:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DROPBOX:NOT_FOUND
- DROPBOX:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DROPBOX:SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DROPBOX:TIMEOUT
- DROPBOX:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DROPBOX:UNAUTHORIZED
- DROPBOX:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## File Requests Delete

`<dropbox:file-requests-delete>`

Deletes a batch of closed file requests. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/file_requests/delete endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- DROPBOX:BAD_REQUEST
- DROPBOX:CLIENT_ERROR
- DROPBOX:CONNECTIVITY
- DROPBOX:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DROPBOX:NOT_FOUND
- DROPBOX:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DROPBOX:SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DROPBOX:TIMEOUT
- DROPBOX:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DROPBOX:UNAUTHORIZED
- DROPBOX:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## File Requests Get

`<dropbox:file-requests-get>`

Returns the specified file request. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/file_requests/get endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- DROPBOX:BAD_REQUEST
- DROPBOX:CLIENT_ERROR
- DROPBOX:CONNECTIVITY
- DROPBOX:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DROPBOX:NOT_FOUND
- DROPBOX:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DROPBOX:SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DROPBOX:TIMEOUT
- DROPBOX:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DROPBOX:UNAUTHORIZED
- DROPBOX:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## File Requests List

`<dropbox:file-requests-list>`

Returns a list of file requests owned by this user. For apps with the app folder permission, this
operation returns only file requests with destinations in the app folder. This operation makes an
HTTP POST request to the /file_requests/list endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- DROPBOX:BAD_REQUEST
- DROPBOX:CLIENT_ERROR
- DROPBOX:CONNECTIVITY
- DROPBOX:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DROPBOX:NOT_FOUND
- DROPBOX:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DROPBOX:SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DROPBOX:TIMEOUT
- DROPBOX:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DROPBOX:UNAUTHORIZED
- DROPBOX:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## File Requests Update

`<dropbox:file-requests-update>`

Updates a file request. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /file_requests/update
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- DROPBOX:BAD_REQUEST
- DROPBOX:CLIENT_ERROR
- DROPBOX:CONNECTIVITY
- DROPBOX:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DROPBOX:NOT_FOUND
- DROPBOX:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DROPBOX:SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DROPBOX:TIMEOUT
- DROPBOX:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DROPBOX:UNAUTHORIZED
- DROPBOX:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Files Copy Batch V2

`<dropbox:files-copy-batch-v2>`

Copies multiple files or folders to different locations simultaneously in the userâs Dropbox. This
operation replaces the `copy_batch:1` route. The **Files Copy Batch V2** operation route returns the
status for each entry, while the `copy_batch:1` route raises failure if any entry fails. The **Files
Copy Batch V2** operation either finishes synchronously, or returns a job ID and finishes
asynchronously. Use the `copy_batch/check:2` route to check the job status. This operation makes an
HTTP POST request to the /files/copy_batch_v2 endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- DROPBOX:BAD_REQUEST
- DROPBOX:CLIENT_ERROR
- DROPBOX:CONNECTIVITY
- DROPBOX:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DROPBOX:NOT_FOUND
- DROPBOX:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DROPBOX:SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DROPBOX:TIMEOUT
- DROPBOX:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DROPBOX:UNAUTHORIZED
- DROPBOX:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Files Copy V2

`<dropbox:files-copy-v2>`

Copies a file or folder to a different location in the userâs Dropbox. If the source path is a
folder, all of the contents are copied. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/files/copy_v2 endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- DROPBOX:BAD_REQUEST
- DROPBOX:CLIENT_ERROR
- DROPBOX:CONNECTIVITY
- DROPBOX:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DROPBOX:NOT_FOUND
- DROPBOX:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DROPBOX:SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DROPBOX:TIMEOUT
- DROPBOX:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DROPBOX:UNAUTHORIZED
- DROPBOX:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Files Create Folder V2

`<dropbox:files-create-folder-v2>`

Creates a folder at a given path. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/files/create_folder_v2 endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- DROPBOX:BAD_REQUEST
- DROPBOX:CLIENT_ERROR
- DROPBOX:CONNECTIVITY
- DROPBOX:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DROPBOX:NOT_FOUND
- DROPBOX:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DROPBOX:SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DROPBOX:TIMEOUT
- DROPBOX:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DROPBOX:UNAUTHORIZED
- DROPBOX:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Files Delete Batch

`<dropbox:files-delete-batch>`

Deletes multiple files or folders simultaneously. This route is asynchronous, as it returns a job ID
immediately and runs the delete batch asynchronously. Use the `delete_batch/check` route to check
the job status. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /files/delete_batch endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- DROPBOX:BAD_REQUEST
- DROPBOX:CLIENT_ERROR
- DROPBOX:CONNECTIVITY
- DROPBOX:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DROPBOX:NOT_FOUND
- DROPBOX:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DROPBOX:SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DROPBOX:TIMEOUT
- DROPBOX:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DROPBOX:UNAUTHORIZED
- DROPBOX:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Files Delete V2

`<dropbox:files-delete-v2>`

Deletes a file or a folder from a given path. If the path is a folder, all of its contents are
deleted too. The response is successful once the file or folder is deleted. The returned metadata
for the item is the corresponding `FileMetadata` or `FolderMetadata` object at the time of deletion,
instead of a `DeletedMetadata` object. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/files/delete_v2 endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- DROPBOX:BAD_REQUEST
- DROPBOX:CLIENT_ERROR
- DROPBOX:CONNECTIVITY
- DROPBOX:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DROPBOX:NOT_FOUND
- DROPBOX:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Files Download

`<dropbox:files-download>`

Downloads a file from a userâs Dropbox. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/files/download endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Dropbox API Arg</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Dropbox API Arg.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Configurations](#configurations)

- DROPBOX:BAD_REQUEST
- DROPBOX:CLIENT_ERROR
- DROPBOX:CONNECTIVITY
- DROPBOX:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- DROPBOX:NOT_FOUND
- DROPBOX:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DROPBOX:SERVER_ERROR
- DROPBOX:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
